---
title: Extracting genes from MB tumour pySCENIC regulons
date: 2023-10-05
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

## Goal of this analysis

To extract the target genes from each MB tumour regulon identified on 2023-05-19.


## Files and directories

### Code

The code can be found in `<pyscenic software directory>/get_genes_in_regulon.py`.

### Output

The output can be found in `<pyscenic results directory>/20231005/`.


## Results

The output contains a CSV file for each regulon that was identified (`<transcription factor>.csv`). Each file contains a column of target genes and a column with the weight of these target genes (which essentially measures how well the TF predicts the target gene; see the SCENIC papers). Genes are sorted from most to least important (highest to lowest weight).

For example, here are the top target genes in the *BRCA1* regulon and their importance.

```{r}
#| eval: true
#| echo: true

library(tidyverse)

brca1 <- read_csv("/CBL_scRNAseq/results/pyscenic/20231005/BRCA1.csv")

head(brca1, 20)
```
